top_signals.title: The Operational Case Against Israel’s Gaza Campaign
top_signals.summary:
Gaza did not have to look the way it looks. That is not a moral claim. It is an operational one.Claims of necessity are invoked to explain the scale of civilian harm, but they founder in the face of operational logic. Supporters of Israeli methods argue that Gaza’s urban battlefield — tunnels, rocket fire, extreme population density, hostages held underground — left no viable alternative to large-scale destruction. That argument rests on a false assumption: that large-scale fires were inevitable rather than chosen, and that the only tradeoff was between more fires and mission failure. That is a false choice,

The post The Operational Case Against Israel’s Gaza Campaign appeared first on War on the Rocks.
top_signals.why_it_matters: Geopolitical escalation affecting commodities, shipping and cross-asset volatility.

top_signals.title: CHART: Copper price surge mints 23 new unicorn mines
top_signals.summary: The ranks of copper mining’s answer to Silicon Valley's $1B startup unicorns swell to 75.
top_signals.why_it_matters: Commodity repricing impacting inflation expectations and industrial supply chains.
